channel.title: Plan Forge Blog — From the Forge
channel.description: Lessons, patterns, and A/B test results from building with AI coding agents. By Scott Nichols.

item.title: The Loop That Never Ends — How Rummag Auto-Smelts Its Own Website Bugs
item.description: A real-world case study: turning a Next.js site audit into a closed-loop execution pipeline. Discovery harness feeds the Crucible, tempering catches regressions, bug registry auto-smelts failures.

item.title: The A/B Test: 99 vs 44 — Same App, Same Model, Same Time
item.description: We built the same .NET app twice: once with Plan Forge guardrails, once with pure vibe coding. Same model. Same time. The quality score: 99/100 vs 44/100. 60 tests vs 13. The data speaks for itself.

item.title: The 80/20 Wall: Why AI Agents Break What They Build
item.description: AI coding agents get you to 80% fast — then start breaking things trying to close the last 20%. The fix isn't a better model. It's a better methodology.

item.title: Spec Kit + Plan Forge: Write the Spec, Enforce the Build
item.description: I started with Spec Kit and loved it. Specification-based planning and agentic coding is like peanut butter and jelly. As my ideas grew, Plan Forge was born. Here's how they work together.

item.title: I Built Guardrails for AI Coding Agents — Here's What I Learned
item.description: After 18 guardrail files, 19 reviewer agents, and 9 tech stack presets, here are the seven hard-won lessons from building Plan Forge — and the mistakes that taught them.

item.title: Quorum Mode: What Happens When 3 AI Models Review Each Other's Code
item.description: We built a feature twice — once with a single model, once with three models in parallel consensus. Both passed all gates. But the code quality difference was measurable.

item.title: From WhatsApp to Shipped PR: How I Automated My Entire Dev Workflow
item.description: Three open-source tools wired into a closed-loop system. Describe a feature from your phone. The system hardens the plan, builds it, captures every decision, reviews independently, and ships.

item.title: One Framework, Seven AI Agents: Why We Stopped Picking Favorites
item.description: Your team uses Copilot, Claude, Cursor, and Gemini. Plan Forge generates native guardrail files for all 7 agents — same quality gates, same pipeline, same 19 reviewer agents. One setup command.

item.title: From Impossible to 7 Minutes: A Year of Building AI Coding Guardrails
item.description: A year ago, getting enterprise-grade code from an AI agent was nearly impossible. Today we build a 99/100 app in 7 minutes. This is the story of that journey.
